error_signature: %error-procasswire: <file>:<n>: procedural assignment to wire, perhaps intended var (ieee <n>-<n> <n>.<n>): <ident> : ... note: in instance <ident> <n> | result_out <= '<n>; | ^~~~~~~~~~ ... for error description see https://verilator.org/warn/procasswire?v=<n>.<n> %error-procasswire: <file>:<n>: procedural assignment to wire, perhaps intended var (ieee <n>-<n> <n>.<n>): <ident> : ... note: in instance <ident> <n> | result_out <= result_temp; | ^~~~~~~~~~ %error-procasswire: <file>:<n>: procedural assignment to wire, perhaps intended var (ieee <n>-<n> <n>.<n>): <ident> : ... note: in instance <ident> <n> | result_valid <= <n>'b0; | ^~~~~~~~~~~~ %error-procasswire: <file>:<n>: procedural assignment to wire, perhaps intended var (ieee <n>-<n> <n>.<n>): <ident> : ... note: in instance <ident> <n> | result_valid <= valid_sr[<n>]; | ^~~~~~~~~~~~ %error: exiting due to <n> error(s) ... see the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance
raw_error_example:
%Error-PROCASSWIRE: output/transposed_4.sv:92:13: Procedural assignment to wire, perhaps intended var (IEEE 1800-2017 6.5): 'result_out'
                                                : ... note: In instance 'transposed_4'
   92 |             result_out <= '0;

                    ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/PROCASSWIRE?v=5.020
%Error-PROCASSWIRE: output/transposed_4.sv:94:13: Procedural assignment to wire, perhaps intended var (IEEE 1800-2017 6.5): 'result_out'
                                                : ... note: In instance 'transposed_4'
   94 |             result_out <= result_temp;
      |             ^~~~~~~~~~
%Error-PROCASSWIRE: output/transposed_4.sv:108:13: Procedural assignment to wire, perhaps intended var (IEEE 1800-2017 6.5): 'result_valid'
                                                 : ... note: In instance 'transposed_4'
  108 |             result_valid <= 1'b0;
      |             ^~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error-PROCASSWIRE: output/transposed_4.sv:110:13: Procedural assignment to wire, perhaps intended var (IEEE 1800-2017 6.5): 'result_valid'
                                                 : ... note: In instance 'transposed_4'
  110 |             result_valid <= valid_sr[1];
      |             ^~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 4 error(s)
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
root_cause: Output ports declared as `wire` cannot be assigned inside procedural blocks (e.g., always_ff or always @(*)).
fix_rule: Declare output ports as `reg` if they are assigned in procedural blocks, or use a separate internal register and assign it to the output wire.
fix_snippet:
output reg signed [15:0] result_out,
output reg result_valid
